title: strychnos innocua var. burtonii
intro: Strychnos innocua var. burtonii, also known as the African strychnine tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Loganiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Strychnos innocua var. burtonii include its ability to treat fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a remedy for snake bites and other poisons. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used in African folk medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
